trigger_reason: step 13 FIRED for R735 (top-3 proximity); step 13.5 v11 adversarial attack triggered.
honest_deviation_label: Step 13.5 spec-attacker is main-context-direct for R735; HONEST DEVIATION POLICY ≤5-spawn budget allocated to R741 attacker (one real spawn) per epoch.

step_13_spec_pre_check_input.rationale: Higher cumulant traces ARE structurally distinct from nuclear norm — they capture skewness/kurtosis. At 144 heads, joint-quantile ranking may be dominated by 1st moment.

attacks.claim: For a low-rank matrix M (which is the typical case for trained attention heads, rank ≈ 8-12 out of d=64), the higher cumulant traces Tr((MM^*)^k) for k=2,3 are monotone functions of the 1st cumulant Tr|M| under realistic spectral distributions. Specifically, for matrices with monotone-decay singular-value spectrum (the empirically observed case per Voita 2019), Tr((MM^*)^k) is dominated by the largest singular value σ_1^{2k}; the joint quantile ranking is essentially σ_1-rank.
attacks.evidence: Empirically, BERT-base attention matrices have singular-value distribution σ_i ≈ σ_1 × (i/d)^{-α} with α ≈ 0.8-1.2 (per Spectral Analysis of Self-Attention 2403.05103 Figure 3). Under this distribution, all moments reduce to a smooth function of σ_1.

attacks.claim: SST-2 dev accuracy at 30% head-prune is dominated by 'is head ANY useful' rather than 'is head MORE useful than next head'. With 144 heads and a typically large set of redundant heads (Michel 2019: ~75% of heads can be pruned without loss), the 30% prune target is well within the redundant pool — any sensible ranking scheme will give similar dev accuracy.
attacks.evidence: Michel 2019 §3: pruning 40-50% of heads at random retains ≥98% of accuracy on similar tasks. At 30% prune, the discrimination signal between A/B/C is below the SST-2 dev noise floor (~0.5 acc-point variation across seeds).

attacks.claim: Paired t-test with 3 seeds (df=2) has ≤20% power for SST-2 accuracy differences < 0.3 points. Given A2's claim, the expected effect size is < 0.2 points, well below power.
attacks.evidence: Power calculation: paired t-test power at df=2 and α=0.05 reaches 0.8 only at effect sizes > 0.7 std. SST-2 across-seed std ≈ 0.15 acc-points; required effect > 0.1 acc-points for significance.

attacks.claim: Voita 2019 is one of several entropy-pruning methods; movement-pruning (2005.07683) and BERT-of-Theseus (2002.02925) are stronger baselines. Outperforming C alone is uninformative.
attacks.evidence: Movement pruning achieves higher SST-2 retention at 30% prune than Voita 2019 by ~0.5 acc-points.

rationale: Two load-bearing attacks succeed (A1: variant equivalence under realistic spectral decay; A2: metric collapse at 30% prune of 144 heads). The pre-check 'uncertain' was the optimistic interpretation; under attack, R735 reduces to a parametrization of nuclear-norm pruning (effectively single-moment) when measured by the proposed metric. The trace-class multi-moment framing does not add empirical content at this scale.
v10_implicit_verdict_for_R735: PASS_WITH_EMPIRICAL_CAVEAT (had step 10 PASSed; v10 would have logged uncertain distinguishability)
v11_verdict_for_R735: FAIL (step 10 FAILed anyway, so the v11-vs-v10 distinction is hypothetical; but the post-attack false verdict demonstrates the v11 channel doing diagnostic work)
notes_for_executor: If executed, the predicted outcome is A ≈ B (paired t-test p > 0.5). The trace-class multi-moment framing requires either (i) a different metric that's sensitive to head-individual-importance not just average accuracy, or (ii) a different scale (small model with sharply different head distribution).
